items.name: ShinyHunters
items.targets: Hundreds of Salesforce customers in mass data theft campaign targeting Experience Cloud configurations
items.ttps: T1190 (Exploit Public-Facing Application), T1213 (Data from Information Repositories), T1530 (Data from Cloud Storage), leveraging modified AuraInspector tool to harvest Salesforce data

items.name: Lazarus Group (North Korea)
items.targets: AllSecure CEO targeted via fake LinkedIn interview, developers targeted via GhostClaw malware
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Spearphishing Attachment), T1204.002 (User Execution: Malicious File), T1547 (Boot or Logon Autostart), social engineering via fake job interviews

items.name: APT28 (Fancy Bear, Russia)
items.targets: Ukrainian military forces in long-term espionage operations, Signal and WhatsApp accounts of government officials globally
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter), T1087 (Account Discovery), advanced custom malware toolkit for cyber-espionage

items.name: MuddyWater (MOIS, Iran)
items.targets: U.S. critical infrastructure and corporate networks using Dindoor backdoor malware
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Phishing), T1059.001 (PowerShell), T1071.001 (Web Protocols for C2), T1105 (Ingress Tool Transfer)

items.name: Qilin Ransomware
items.targets: RWB Consulting Engineers, U.S. electric cooperative purported breach
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1083 (File and Directory Discovery), T1567 (Exfiltration Over Web Service)

items.name: Akira Ransomware
items.targets: netCOMPONENTS and multiple victims in ongoing ransomware operations
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter), T1021 (Remote Services)

items.name: Everest Ransomware
items.targets: First Priority Group compromised in recent attack
items.ttps: T1486 (Data Encrypted for Impact), T1490 (Inhibit System Recovery), T1048 (Exfiltration Over Alternative Protocol)

items.name: Iranian Threat Actors
items.targets: Psychological warfare operations with fake shelter danger calls, critical infrastructure targeting
items.ttps: T1566 (Phishing), T1498 (Network Denial of Service), T1589 (Gather Victim Identity Information), psychological operations and GPS jamming near Iran

items.name: Chinese Nexus Actors
items.targets: Qatar government and organizations with PlugX malware tied to Middle East Iranian conflict
items.ttps: T1566.001 (Spearphishing Attachment), T1059 (Command and Scripting Interpreter), T1027 (Obfuscated Files), geopolitical targeting with PlugX RAT

items.name: BeatBanker Android Trojan
items.targets: Android banking users via dual-mode trojan with phishing capabilities
items.ttps: T1417 (Input Capture), T1523 (Evade Analysis Environment), T1566.001 (Phishing), overlay attacks on banking applications

items.name: SurxRAT Android Malware
items.targets: Android users with LLM-powered phishing and data theft capabilities
items.ttps: T1417 (Input Capture), T1636 (Protected User Data), T1566 (Phishing), T1412 (Capture SMS Messages), AI-enhanced social engineering

items.name: Tycoon 2FA PhaaS
items.targets: Multiple organizations via phishing-as-a-service platform (disrupted by law enforcement)
items.ttps: T1539 (Steal Web Session Cookie), T1566.002 (Spearphishing Link), T1111 (Two-Factor Authentication Interception), adversary-in-the-middle attacks

canada_landscape.summary: Canadian entities appeared in multiple threat intelligence reports over the past 48 hours. Meta disabled 150K accounts linked to Southeast Asia scam centers affecting Canada among other nations. A phishing campaign abusing .arpa domains specifically targeted Canadian entities, and alleged Canadian data listings surfaced on dark web forums including AT&T Canada and Canva data.
canada_landscape.retailers: None identified - no specific incidents affecting Canadian Tire, SportChek, Mark's or other major Canadian retailers in the reporting period
canada_landscape.financial: None identified - no direct incidents affecting major Canadian financial institutions (CIBC, RBC, TD, Scotiabank, BMO) reported in the past 48 hours, though general phishing and fraud campaigns may impact Canadian banking customers
